dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9067 IH: To amend the Communications Act of 1934 to provide for a gender identity content descriptor for video programming, and for other purposes.
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Moore of Alabama introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce

form.official-title: To amend the Communications Act of 1934 to provide for a gender identity content descriptor for video programming, and for other purposes.

section.header: Gender identity content descriptor for video programming
section.text: Title VII of the Communications Act of 1934 (47 U.S.C. 601 et seq.) is amended by adding at the end the following:

section.header: Gender identity content descriptor for video programming

subsection.text: Not later than 90 days after the date of the enactment of this section, the Commission shall—

paragraph.text: determine if the conditions of subsection (b) have been met; and
if the Commission determines under paragraph (1) that such conditions have not been met, promulgate regulations that—

subparagraph.text: establish a content descriptor for video programming that depicts, discusses, or promotes gender identity, gender transition, transgender identity, or non-binary identity; and
require distributors of video programming to transmit the content descriptor described in subparagraph (A)—

subclause.text: contains the content described in such subparagraph; and
is marketed to children (such as programming that is rated TV–Y, TV–Y7, or TV–G under the TV Parental Guidelines); and

clause.text: in a manner that, to the maximum extent technically feasible, can be used with the feature described in section 303(x) (commonly known as the V-chip) and other parental-control technologies.

subsection.text: The conditions of this subsection are the following:

paragraph.text: The TV Parental Guidelines have been revised so as to include a content descriptor for video programming that depicts, discusses, or promotes gender identity, gender transition, transgender identity, or non-binary identity.
The content descriptor described in paragraph (1) is being consistently transmitted with the rating under the TV Parental Guidelines for video programming that—

subparagraph.text: contains content described in such paragraph; and
is rated TV–Y, TV–Y7, or TV–G under the TV Parental Guidelines or is otherwise marketed to children.

subsection.text: Nothing in this section may be construed to ban or censor any video programming.

In this section, the term TV Parental Guidelines means the voluntary ratings (or any successor to such ratings) for video programming maintained by the TV Parental Guidelines Monitoring Board (or any successor to such Board).
